docs.title: Personal budget documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the documentation for this local-first personal budget project: manual expense logging in CSV, budget-month rules, text summaries, and an interactive dashboard.

For folder paths see Where data lives. For step-by-step procedures see Workflows.

docs.title: End-to-end data flow
docs.text:
The diagram runs from capture (adding rows to CSV) through shared loading to reports (text summaries and HTML dashboard). Click a box in the diagram (desktop) or use the step reference table below.

Versioning: keep data/expenses.csv as the single source of truth; generated files under reports/ can be regenerated at any time — do not treat them as the only copy of your data.

flowchart TB
  DMP["⓪ Budget conventions"]

  subgraph CAP["① Capture"]
    ADD["Add expense rows"]
    CSV["data/expenses.csv"]
  end

  subgraph LOAD["② Shared loading"]
    ED["expense_data.py"]
    BM["Budget month mapping"]
  end

  subgraph OUT["③ Reporting"]
    SUM["monthly_summary.py"]
    DASH["expense_dashboard.py"]
    TXT["reports/*.txt"]
    HTML["reports/dashboard.html"]
  end

  subgraph VIEW["④ Review"]
    TERM["Terminal summary"]
    BROWSER["Browser dashboard"]
    DOCS["This documentation site"]
  end

  DMP --> ADD
  ADD --> CSV
  CSV --> ED
  ED --> BM
  ED --> SUM
  ED --> DASH
  SUM --> TXT
  DASH --> HTML
  TXT --> TERM
  HTML --> BROWSER
  DOCS --> VIEW

  click DMP "rules/budget-month/" "Budget month rule"
  click ADD "workflows/add-expenses/" "Add expenses workflow"
  click CSV "where-data-lives/" "Where data lives"
  click ED "systems/expense-data/" "Expense data module"
  click SUM "workflows/monthly-summary/" "Monthly summary workflow"
  click DASH "workflows/dashboard/" "Dashboard workflow"
  click HTML "dashboard.html" "Interactive dashboard"

docs.title: Step reference (clickable links)
docs.text: Step What happens Documentation ⓪ Budget conventions 21st–20th budget months; category naming Budget month rule ① Add expense rows One CSV row per purchase, positive amounts Add expenses data/expenses.csv Single ledger file on disk Where data lives expense_data.py Parse CSV, validate fields, compute budget month Expense data monthly_summary.py Category totals for one or all budget months Monthly summary expense_dashboard.py Build interactive HTML with charts and metrics Dashboard workflow reports/*.txt Optional saved terminal output Where data lives reports/dashboard.html Charts, KPIs, recent transactions Interactive dashboard

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This project currently spans:

  • Capture — manual rows in data/expenses.csv
  • Classification — free-form categories and budget month assignment
  • Summaries — terminal reports via monthly_summary.py
  • Visualization — HTML dashboard via expense_dashboard.py
  • Documentation — this site (Material for MkDocs), modeled after the NMCB hand-over documentation

There is no database, no cloud sync, and no Python package dependencies for the core scripts.

docs.title: Architecture principle
docs.text:
One source of truth. Every report and chart should trace back to data/expenses.csv. Generated outputs under reports/ are disposable; the CSV is not.

Every workflow should make it possible to answer:

  • Where did this amount come from?
  • Which budget month does this expense belong to?
  • Can I regenerate this report from the CSV alone?

docs.title: Keep in mind (privacy and backups)
docs.text: These habits apply to every export, backup, or share of budget data.

docs.title: Minimize what you share
docs.text:
• Share only what someone actually needs (e.g. category totals, not full descriptions).
  • Prefer aggregates over row-level detail when discussing spending with others.

docs.title: Identifiers and notes
docs.text:
• The description and notes columns may contain personal or sensitive details — treat the CSV like private financial data.
  • Do not commit the CSV to a public repository unless you are comfortable exposing that information.

docs.title: Before you copy or upload anything
docs.text:
1. Is each column required for the purpose?
  2. Could any field be replaced by a less detailed alternative?
  3. Is the copy stored in a location you control (local disk, private repo)?

docs.title: How this site is organised
docs.text: Section Purpose Where data lives Paths for CSV, reports, and scripts Site usage How to run and publish this documentation Workflows Add expenses, summaries, dashboard generation Systems Python modules and script reference Rules Budget month (21st–20th) convention Dashboard (live) Interactive charts (generated before site build)

docs.title: How to use this documentation
docs.text:
Use the diagram above, then Where data lives and the workflow or system page for the task at hand.

Each workflow page answers: why it exists; when to do it; where files live; steps; what to check before you are done.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
# Terminal summary for May budget month
python3 scripts/monthly_summary.py --month 2026-05

# Regenerate dashboard and open it
python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --open

# Serve this documentation locally
pip install -r requirements-docs.txt
python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --output docs/dashboard.html
mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Site usage
docs.text: This documentation site is built with Material for MkDocs, the same stack used by the NMCB hand-over documentation.

docs.title: Local preview
docs.text:
Install doc dependencies (one-time):

pip install -r requirements-docs.txt

Regenerate the embedded dashboard page, then serve:

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --output docs/dashboard.html
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000. The site reloads when you edit files under docs/.

docs.title: Build static site
docs.text:
python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --output docs/dashboard.html
mkdocs build

Output is written to site/. Open site/index.html or deploy the folder to any static host.

docs.title: Publish to GitHub Pages
docs.text:
Pushing to master runs .github/workflows/docs.yml, which:

  1. Installs MkDocs Material
  2. Generates docs/dashboard.html from the current CSV
  3. Deploys to GitHub Pages

After the workflow completes, the site is available at:

https://sxzhang1201.github.io/personal-budget/

Enable Pages in the repo settings if needed: Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions.

docs.title: Edit content
docs.text:
Change Edit Home page / data-flow diagram docs/index.md Navigation mkdocs.yml → nav Workflow pages docs/workflows/*.md Theme colours mkdocs.yml → theme.palette

Mermaid diagrams use fenced code blocks:

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A --> B
```

docs.title: Relationship to the dashboard
docs.text:
Artifact Location Audience Documentation site docs/ → site/ How the project works Interactive dashboard reports/dashboard.html Spending charts and KPIs

The nav link Dashboard (live) opens the generated HTML chart page inside the doc site bundle.

docs.title: Where data lives
docs.text:
All project data and outputs use paths relative to the repository root.

personal-budget/
├── data/
│   └── expenses.csv          # Source of truth — one row per expense
├── reports/
│   ├── dashboard.html        # Generated interactive dashboard
│   ├── expense_summary_all.txt
│   ├── monthly_report_2026-05.txt
│   └── sample_monthly_report_2026-06.txt
├── scripts/
│   ├── expense_data.py       # Shared CSV loading and budget month logic
│   ├── expense_dashboard.py  # Dashboard generator
│   ├── monthly_summary.py    # Terminal category summaries
│   └── dashboard_template.html
├── docs/                     # MkDocs source (this site)
└── mkdocs.yml

docs.title: data/expenses.csv
docs.text: Property Value Role Single ledger for all expenses Format CSV with header row Edited by You (manually or via spreadsheet) Read by All Python scripts via expense_data.load_expenses()

docs.title: Columns
docs.text:
Column Required Example date Yes 2026-06-01 category Yes Groceries description No AH (Almere) amount Yes 35.70 payment_method No Debit card notes No Optional memo

Do not overwrite without backup

There is no automatic history. Copy or commit the CSV before bulk edits.

docs.title: reports/
docs.text:
Generated and optional saved outputs. Safe to delete and recreate except if you stored notes there you care about.

File Created by Purpose dashboard.html expense_dashboard.py Interactive charts and metrics *.txt Manual copy of terminal output Archived summaries

Regenerate the dashboard:

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py

For this documentation site, the dashboard is also copied to docs/dashboard.html before mkdocs build.

docs.title: scripts/
docs.text:
Python entry points and shared modules. See Systems for detail.

Script Input Output monthly_summary.py CSV stdout (terminal table) expense_dashboard.py CSV HTML file expense_data.py CSV Python Expense objects (imported, not run directly)

docs.title: What is not stored here
docs.text:
• No database files
  • No cloud credentials
  • No automatic bank import

Everything stays on your machine unless you push the repo to GitHub or copy files elsewhere.

docs.title: Budget month rule
docs.text:
Each budget month runs from the 21st of one calendar month through the 20th of the next.

This matches a common pay-cycle or billing-period mental model: late-month spending counts toward the month you are "in" financially.

docs.title: Mapping rule
docs.text: Expense date (day of month) Budget month 21 – 31 (or last day) Same calendar month 1 – 20 Previous calendar month

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Expense date Budget month 2026-05-25 2026-05 2026-05-20 2026-04 2026-06-05 2026-05 2026-06-21 2026-06 
flowchart LR
  subgraph May["Calendar May"]
    M1["1–20 → Apr budget"]
    M2["21–31 → May budget"]
  end
  subgraph Jun["Calendar June"]
    J1["1–20 → May budget"]
    J2["21–30 → Jun budget"]
  end

docs.title: Where it is applied
docs.text:
Component Behaviour expense_data.budget_month() Core mapping function monthly_summary.py --month Filters by budget month Dashboard dropdown Groups and filters by budget month

The date column in CSV always stores the actual transaction date. The budget month is computed — never stored as a separate column.

docs.title: Practical impact
docs.text:
For May budget month (--month 2026-05), you include:

  • All expenses dated May 21 – May 31
  • All expenses dated June 1 – June 20

June budget month starts with expenses dated June 21 onward.

docs.title: Implementation reference
docs.text:
def budget_month(expense_date: datetime) -> str:
    if expense_date.day > 20:
        return expense_date.strftime("%Y-%m")
    # ... previous calendar month

See scripts/expense_data.py for the full function.

docs.title: Systems
docs.text:
Python modules and scripts that power summaries and the dashboard.

Component Role Expense data Shared CSV parsing and budget month logic Scripts CLI entry points 
flowchart LR
  CSV["expenses.csv"] --> ED["expense_data.py"]
  ED --> MS["monthly_summary.py"]
  ED --> DB["expense_dashboard.py"]
  DB --> TPL["dashboard_template.html"]
  TPL --> HTML["dashboard.html"]

All scripts are stdlib-only — no pip install required for reporting.

docs.title: Expense data module
docs.text: scripts/expense_data.py is the shared library for reading and classifying expenses.

docs.title: Why it exists
docs.text: Both monthly_summary.py and expense_dashboard.py need the same parsing rules and budget month logic. Centralizing avoids drift.

docs.title: Expense record
docs.text:
Each CSV row becomes a frozen dataclass:

Field Type Notes date datetime Parsed from YYYY-MM-DD category str Required description str May be empty amount Decimal Exact money arithmetic payment_method str Defaults to "Unknown" if blank notes str May be empty

Property expense.budget_month returns the YYYY-MM budget month string.

docs.title: budget_month(expense_date)
docs.text: Maps a calendar date to a budget month (21st through 20th rule). Used by summaries, dashboard, and tests.

docs.title: load_expenses(csv_path)
docs.text:
• Validates required columns: date, category, amount
  • Skips completely blank rows
  • Raises SystemExit with row numbers on invalid dates or amounts
  • Returns expenses sorted by date, then category

docs.title: Usage in other scripts
docs.text:
from expense_data import load_expenses, budget_month

for expense in load_expenses(csv_path):
    print(expense.budget_month, expense.category, expense.amount)

Run scripts from the repo root or scripts/ directory so the import resolves.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Where data lives — CSV columns
  • Budget month rule

docs.title: Scripts
docs.text: Command-line tools in scripts/.

docs.title: monthly_summary.py
docs.text:
Purpose: Print category totals to the terminal.

python3 scripts/monthly_summary.py [--month YYYY-MM] [--csv PATH]
Flag Default Description --month all months Filter by budget month --csv data/expenses.csv Input file

Workflow: Monthly summary

docs.title: expense_dashboard.py
docs.text:
Purpose: Generate interactive HTML dashboard.

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py [--output PATH] [--csv PATH] [--open]
Flag Default Description --output reports/dashboard.html HTML destination --csv data/expenses.csv Input file --open off Open browser after write

Uses dashboard_template.html and embeds expense JSON for client-side charts.

Workflow: Dashboard

docs.title: dashboard_template.html
docs.text:
Not run directly. HTML/CSS/JS template with a __DASHBOARD_DATA__ placeholder replaced by expense_dashboard.py.

Chart library: Chart.js (CDN).

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Script Python packages monthly_summary.py stdlib only expense_dashboard.py stdlib only MkDocs site see requirements-docs.txt

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text:
Step-by-step procedures for day-to-day budget tracking.

Workflow When to use Add expenses After each purchase or batch import Monthly summary Quick category totals in the terminal Dashboard Charts, trends, and richer metrics

All workflows read from the same file: data/expenses.csv.

Budget month filtering is shared — see Budget month rule.

docs.title: Why this exists
docs.text: Every report and chart depends on rows in data/expenses.csv. This workflow keeps the ledger complete and consistently formatted.

docs.title: When to do it
docs.text:
• After a purchase you want to track
  • When reconciling bank or card statements (batch entry)

docs.title: Where
docs.text: Item Path Ledger data/expenses.csv

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Open data/expenses.csv in a text editor or spreadsheet.
  2. Add one row per expense at the bottom (keep the header row unchanged).
  3. Set date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
  4. Choose a category (consistent spelling helps summaries).
  5. Enter a positive amount.
  6. Optionally fill description, payment_method, and notes.
  7. Save the file.

docs.title: Example row
docs.text: 2026-06-08,Groceries,AH (Almere),42.15,Debit card,

docs.title: What to check before done
docs.text:
• [ ] Date parses as YYYY-MM-DD
  • [ ] category and amount are not empty
  • [ ] amount is positive (no minus sign)
  • [ ] You know which budget month the date falls into

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
python3 scripts/monthly_summary.py --month 2026-05
python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --open

docs.title: Why this exists
docs.text: The terminal summary shows category totals only. The dashboard adds trends, payment-method splits, month-over-month change, and a recent transactions table.

docs.title: When to do it
docs.text:
• Monthly review with visuals
  • After adding many new rows
  • Before publishing the documentation site (dashboard is embedded in docs)

docs.title: Where
docs.text: Input Output data/expenses.csv reports/dashboard.html (default)

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
Generate the dashboard:

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py

Generate and open in your browser:

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --open

Custom output path:

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --output reports/dashboard.html

For MkDocs, copy into docs/ before building the site:

python3 scripts/expense_dashboard.py --output docs/dashboard.html

docs.title: Metrics (selected budget month)
docs.text: Metric Description Total spend Sum of all expenses in the budget month Transactions Count and average transaction size Daily average Total ÷ 30 (budget month length) Top category Highest-spend category Largest expense Single biggest row Month over month % change vs previous budget month Fixed / variable Fixed = Mortgage, Insurance, Utilities, Membership

docs.title: Charts
docs.text:
• Monthly spending trend
  • Category bar chart and pie chart
  • Payment method donut
  • Stacked category breakdown by budget month

Use the Budget month dropdown to switch periods.

Internet required

Charts load Chart.js from a CDN. Offline viewing shows layout but not graphs.

docs.title: What to check before done
docs.text:
• [ ] Budget month dropdown lists expected months
  • [ ] Fixed costs (mortgage, utilities) appear in the selected month
  • [ ] Regenerated after CSV edits

docs.title: Live page
docs.text: Open the Interactive dashboard from this site (generated at build time).

docs.title: Why this exists
docs.text: Fast category totals in the terminal without opening a browser — useful for quick checks or copying into reports/*.txt.

docs.title: When to do it
docs.text:
• End of a budget month review
  • Before updating archived text reports

docs.title: Where
docs.text: Input Output data/expenses.csv Printed table on stdout

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
Summarize all expenses (every budget month combined):

python3 scripts/monthly_summary.py

Summarize one budget month:

python3 scripts/monthly_summary.py --month 2026-05

Use a custom CSV path:

python3 scripts/monthly_summary.py --csv path/to/expenses.csv --month 2026-05

docs.title: Example output
docs.text:
Expense summary for 2026-05
===========================
Category       Amount
-------------  ----------
Dining         $   112.75
Groceries      $   215.62
...
Total          $ 2,524.80

docs.title: What to check before done
docs.text:
• [ ] --month uses budget month, not calendar month of each row
  • [ ] Totals match expectations for large fixed items (mortgage, insurance)
  • [ ] Optional: save output to reports/monthly_report_YYYY-MM.txt

docs.text:
